source: CCB Guide sur les notifications NIS2 v1.3 (August 2025)

significant_incident_criteria.description: Five categories of significant events (non-exhaustive). Any one triggers mandatory notification.

malicious_compromise.description: Suspected malicious compromise of CIA — unauthorized access, unauthorized configuration, unauthorized execution

availability.description: Availability compromise causing severe operational disruption

thresholds.description: ≥20% of users cannot access service for ≥1 hour
Users lose access for ≥1 hour and entity cannot determine number affected

additional_triggers: Loss or disclosure of intellectual property compromising future revenues
Exfiltration of trade secrets per Directive (EU) 2016/943

third_party_damage.description: Material, physical, or moral damage to other persons
triggers: substantial financial consequences for third parties

recurring_events.description: Multiple non-significant incidents collectively becoming significant
recurring_events.note: Cannot be evaluated from a single incident assessment — flag for analyst awareness

notification_timeline.note: 'Without undue delay' means as soon as possible, not waiting for the deadline maximum

dora_entities.description: Banking and financial market infrastructure entities under DORA are excluded from NIS2 notification (Art. 6, §3)

ir_entities.description: 11 digital infrastructure/service provider types covered by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/7151 — IR thresholds prevail over CCB horizontal thresholds
ir_entities.note: Handled by three-tier router (IR tier takes precedence)
